chinese_text: 瞻彼旱麓，榛楛濟濟。豈弟君子，干祿豈弟。
translation_text:
Look at the foot of the Han,
How abundantly grow the hazel and arrow-thorn.
Easy and self-possessed was our prince,
In his pursuit of dignity (still) easy and self-possessed.

chinese_text: 瑟彼玉瓚，黃流在中。豈弟君子，福祿攸降。
translation_text:
Massive is that libation-cup of jade,
With the
​yellow liquid sparkling in it.
Easy and self-possessed was our prince,
The fit recipient of blessing and dignity.

chinese_text: 鳶飛戾天，魚躍于淵。豈弟君子，遐不作人。
translation_text:
The hawk flies up to heaven,
The fishes leap in the deep.
Easy and self-possessed was our prince:—
Did
he not exert an influence on men?

chinese_text: 清酒既載，騂牡既備。以享以祀，以介景福。
translation_text:
His clear spirits were in the vessels;
His red bull was ready;—
To
offer, to sacrifice,
To increase his bright happiness.

chinese_text: 瑟彼柞棫，民所燎矣。豈弟君子，神所勞矣。
translation_text:
Thick grow the oaks and the buckthorn,
Which the people use for fuel.
Easy and self-possessed was our prince,
Cheered and encouraged by the spirits.

chinese_text: 莫莫葛藟，施于條枚。豈弟君子，求福不回。
translation_text:
Luxuriant are the dolichos and other creepers,
Clinging to the branches and stems.
Easy and self-possessed was our prince,
Seeking for happiness by no crooked ways.
